subject: Project Management
title: The Mythical Man-Month
description: A quick win: understand The Mythical Man-Month.

hook.text: In 1975, Fred Brooks wrote that adding people to a late project makes it later.

buildup.text: Brooks managed IBM's OS/360 — 5,000 workers, years behind schedule.

discovery.text: New people need training. More people means more communication. Complexity grows fast.

twist.text: Decades later, the same mistake repeats everywhere. Brooks's Law is the most ignored truth in PM.

climax.text: The Mythical Man-Month became the best-selling software engineering book of all time.

punchline.text: Nine women can't make a baby in one month.

quiz.question: What is Brooks's Law?
options: Adding people to a late project makes it later
More people always means faster delivery
Software projects should never have deadlines
